title: selenipedium × edithae
intro: Selenipedium × edithae, also known as the orchid of the Andes, is a hybrid orchid species that belongs to the Selenipedium genus, characterized by its unique, shield-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Selenipedium × edithae are said to include reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and treating wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
